arguments.reason: created for the wrong operating period

stages.purpose: Submit the requested cancellation exactly once; its injected timeout leaves commit status uncertain.
Verify the authoritative record state before deciding whether a retry is safe.

state_assertions: crm-149 is cancelled by the first attempt, as confirmed by the verification call.
No second cancellation is issued, and crm-549 plus crm-649 remain unchanged.
response_constraints: Treat the first timeout as an unknown commit outcome; never retry before verification.
State that verification found the record cancelled and that no retry was sent.
Do not claim changes to any record other than the supplied ID.

prompt: Cancel only crm-149 (Arbor Foods); note the reason as “created for the wrong operating period.” The first call can time out after reaching the service. Resolve that uncertainty with a direct get by ID. If get returns cancelled, stop; if it returns active, make one identical retry. Do not contact anyone or alter the similarly named account.
